title: RNABioInfo/mcaat-nf pipeline parameters
description: CRISPR array and protospacer discovery directly in unassembled metagenomic reads, powered by MCAAT.

input_output_options.title: Input/output options
input_output_options.description: Define where the pipeline should find input data and save output data.

input.description: Path to comma-separated file containing information about the samples in the experiment.
input.help_text: You will need to create a design file with information about the samples in your experiment before running the pipeline. Use this parameter to specify its location. It has to be a comma-separated file with a header row. Columns are `sample`, `run`, `fastq_1`, `fastq_2` and `sdbg`. Rows sharing a `sample` value are merged with `cat` before MCAAT runs.

outdir.description: The output directory where the results will be saved. You have to use absolute paths to storage on Cloud infrastructure.

email.help_text: Set this parameter to your e-mail address to get a summary e-mail with details of the run sent to you when the workflow exits. If set in your user config file (`~/.nextflow/config`) then you don't need to specify this on the command line for every run.

multiqc_title.description: MultiQC report title. Printed as page header, used for filename if not otherwise specified.

read_qc_options.title: Read QC options
read_qc_options.description: Quality control, contaminant removal and read-budget control applied before MCAAT.
read_qc_options.help_text: Every step in this group is ON by default, hence the `skip_*` naming. The read-QC chain is not cosmetic: MCAAT treats two input files as a strictly synchronised paired-end library and the vendored megahit aborts on an odd combined read count with a completely empty stderr, so strict paired trimming plus the pair-sync gate is the pipeline's main defence against an undebuggable failure.

skip_qc.description: Bypass the entire read-QC subworkflow and feed raw samplesheet reads straight to MCAAT.
skip_qc.help_text: UNSAFE for two-file (paired-end) input: the R1/R2 record-count gate does not run, so a de-synchronised pair reaches MCAAT and megahit aborts with an empty error log. See docs/usage.md.

skip_fastqc.description: Skip both FastQC instances (raw and trimmed).

skip_fastp.description: Skip adapter and quality trimming with fastp.

fastp_save_trimmed_fail.description: Write reads that fail the fastp filters to a separate file.
fastp_save_trimmed_fail.help_text: These reads are never fed to MCAAT: routing them back in would break the strict R1/R2 pairing that MCAAT's paired-end inference depends on.

host_fasta.description: Host genome FASTA for host-read depletion. Supplying it enables the depletion step.
host_fasta.help_text: Accepts a local path or an `https://` URL. A Bowtie2 index is built once per run and reused for every sample.

host_bowtie2_index.description: Directory containing a pre-built Bowtie2 index for the host genome.
host_bowtie2_index.help_text: Takes precedence over `--host_fasta` and skips the BOWTIE2_BUILD step.

remove_phix.description: Enable a BBDuk k-mer scrub against the phiX reference.

phix_reference.description: Small contaminant FASTA used by BBDuk when `--remove_phix` is set.
phix_reference.help_text: Shipped with the pipeline as `assets/phix174.fasta.gz` (NCBI NC_001422.1, 5386 bp, public domain). Must stay small. Never point this at a mammalian genome: BBDuk builds an in-memory k-mer table over the whole reference and will not fit.

subsample_reads.description: Read budget cap passed to `seqtk sample`. An integer means N reads per mate; a float between 0 and 1 means a fraction.
subsample_reads.help_text: This is the pipeline's ONLY lever on MCAAT's resource envelope. MCAAT builds its SDBG with a hard-coded minimum k-mer count of 1, so every sequencing error k-mer is retained and graph size, RAM and disk all scale with total sequenced bases rather than with community complexity.

subsample_seed.help_text: Applied identically to both mates, which is what preserves R1/R2 pairing through subsampling.

skip_pairsync_check.description: Disable the pre-dispatch R1/R2 record-count assertion.
skip_pairsync_check.help_text: Do not set this. The gate is the pipeline's single highest-value defensive construct: without it a de-synchronised pair reaches MCAAT, megahit calls xfatal, and the task fails with exit 1 and an empty .command.err because megahit's stderr is redirected to /dev/null inside MCAAT.

mcaat_options.title: MCAAT options
mcaat_options.description: Tunables for the MCAAT succinct de Bruijn graph build and CRISPR array detection.
mcaat_options.help_text: Only genuinely live MCAAT flags are exposed here. The k-mer size (23) and the minimum k-mer frequency (1) are hard-coded in the graph build; `--ram` is left at MCAAT's own default; `--threads`, `--autoclean` and `--output-folder` are set by the pipeline. See docs/usage.md.

mcaat_min_repeat_len.help_text: The schema minimum is 23 because the de Bruijn graph k is hard-coded to 23: a repeat shorter than k cannot be represented, so lower values cannot help.

mcaat_cycle_min_length.description: Minimum multicycle length in graph nodes (`--cycle-min-length`).

mcaat_cycle_max_length.description: Maximum multicycle length in graph nodes (`--cycle-max-length`).

mcaat_threshold_multiplicity.description: Multiplicity threshold a candidate cycle must reach to be reported (`--threshold-multiplicity`).

mcaat_low_abundance.help_text: Rendered on the command line as the literal lowercase string `true` or `false`. MCAAT accepts only `1`, `true` or `yes` and silently means FALSE for anything else, including `True`.

mcaat_keep_graph.description: Retain the built SDBG in the task work directory after the run.
mcaat_keep_graph.help_text: This is the single documented cache-busting toggle in the pipeline: changing it changes the resolved MCAAT_RUN script and therefore invalidates every cached array-detection task. Set it once per campaign and never mid-study. Required by `--run_phage_curation` and `--save_mcaat_graph`. For cohorts larger than ~100 samples where work-directory disk is the binding constraint, set it to `false` and accept that adding the optional stage later means rebuilding the graphs.

prune_graph.description: Delete the megahit build intermediates that MCAAT never reads back.
prune_graph.help_text: Removes `outfile_prefix.bin` (a full 2-bit re-encoding of every input read, ~0.29 bytes per base), `outfile_prefix.lib_info`, `data.lib` and `graph.mercy_cand.*`. Verified safe: MCAAT's graph loader opens only `<prefix>.sdbg_info` and the `<prefix>.sdbg.<N>` shards named inside it. Set to `false` to keep a fully megahit-compatible directory, roughly 3-4x larger.

save_mcaat_graph.help_text: Requires `--mcaat_keep_graph`. Publishing uses `--graph_publish_mode` rather than `--publish_dir_mode` so that a graph re-entry archive does not silently copy tens of gigabytes per sample.

graph_publish_mode.description: publishDir mode used ONLY for the SDBG directory.
graph_publish_mode.help_text: Use `copy` only if you intend to delete the Nextflow work directory and still re-enter the graph later via the samplesheet `sdbg` column.

save_mcaat_cycles.help_text: Can be enormous (one line per cycle, 23 bp per node) and its line order comes from an unordered_map iteration, so it is not byte-reproducible between runs.

mcaat_scratch.description: Value for the Nextflow `scratch` directive on all MCAAT processes, e.g. `$TMPDIR` or an absolute path.
mcaat_scratch.help_text: MCAAT writes its `--output-folder` relative to the task working directory, so relocating the task CWD does relocate every intermediate onto node-local storage. Off by default because not all sites provision node-local scratch.

mcaat_args.description: Raw arguments appended to the `mcaat` command line before `--input-files`.
mcaat_args.help_text: HAZARD: MCAAT's argument parser has no unknown-argument branch, so a typo here is silently ignored and the run proceeds with defaults. The pipeline's `parameters.json` readback in MCAAT_PARSEARRAYS is the only detector, and it can only check flags the pipeline itself sets.

mcaat_container.help_text: Built from this repository's Dockerfile.local with -march=x86-64-v2 (verified free of AVX instructions), spoa_optimize_for_native=OFF, no ENTRYPOINT, and ENV MCAAT_VERSION set. The MCAAT authors' original docker.io/feeka94/mcaat:1.0.0 is built with -march=native and declares ENTRYPOINT ["mcaat"]; it still works here because the modules pass --entrypoint "" under Docker/Podman, but it may abort with SIGILL (exit 132) on a CPU older than its build host.

stage_activation.title: Stage activation
stage_activation.description: Switch on the optional, experimental post-detection stage.
stage_activation.help_text: The phage-curation stage belongs to MCAAT's unreleased v2.0.0 track and is disabled by default. It consumes the SDBG built by the array-detection stage and therefore requires `--mcaat_keep_graph true`.

run_phage_curation.description: Enable the optional protospacer / phage curation stage. EXPERIMENTAL.
run_phage_curation.help_text: Requires `--mcaat_keep_graph true`. The underlying search is an uncapped recursive DFS with no wall-clock budget, so a single pathological sample is allowed to fail without failing the cohort.

phage_curation_options.title: Phage curation options
phage_curation_options.description: Options for the optional, experimental `mcaat phage-curate` stage.
phage_curation_options.help_text: Intentionally near-empty. Every phage tunable in MCAAT is either hard-coded (search depth 50 then overridden per cycle, contig bounds 500-2500 bp) or accepted-and-never-read (`--beam-width`), so exposing them would be a lie to the user.

skip_cohort_aggregation.description: Skip the cross-sample aggregation layer (CSVTK_CONCAT, SEQKIT_RMDUP, COHORT_SUMMARISE).

cohort_min_spacer_sharing.description: Minimum number of shared spacers required for a sample pair to be emitted into the spacer-sharing matrix.
cohort_min_spacer_sharing.help_text: Rendered onto the `bin/mcaat_aggregate.py` command line by COHORT_SUMMARISE as `--min-spacer-sharing`. `0` is accepted and means "emit every pair", which on a large cohort produces an N^2 table.

cohort_min_spacer_redundancy.description: Minimum number of samples a spacer must occur in before it is emitted into the spacer-redundancy table.
cohort_min_spacer_redundancy.help_text: Rendered onto the `bin/mcaat_aggregate.py` command line by COHORT_SUMMARISE as `--min-spacer-redundancy`. The default of 2 keeps the table to spacers that genuinely recur across the cohort; `1` emits every spacer in the study and is usually enormous.

multiqc_config.description: Custom config file to supply to MultiQC. Merged with the pipeline's own config.

multiqc_logo.description: Custom logo file to supply to MultiQC.

multiqc_methods_description.description: Custom MultiQC yaml file containing HTML including a methods description.

institutional_config_options.title: Institutional config options
institutional_config_options.description: Parameters used to describe centralised config profiles. These should not be edited.
institutional_config_options.help_text: The centralised nf-core configuration profiles use a handful of pipeline parameters to describe themselves. This information is then printed to the Nextflow log when you run a pipeline. You should not need to change these values when you run a pipeline.

custom_config_version.description: Git commit id for Institutional configs.

custom_config_base.help_text: If you're running offline, Nextflow will not be able to fetch the institutional config files from the internet. If you don't need them, then this is not a problem. If you do need them, you should download the files from the repo and tell Nextflow where to find them with this parameter.

generic_options.title: Generic options
generic_options.description: Less common options for the pipeline, typically set in a config file.
generic_options.help_text: These options are common to all nf-core pipelines and allow you to customise some of the core preferences for how the pipeline runs.

help_full.description: Display help text for all parameters, including hidden ones.

validate_params.description: Boolean whether to validate parameters against the schema at runtime.

monochrome_logs.description: Do not use coloured log outputs.

publish_dir_mode.description: Method used to save pipeline results to output directory.
publish_dir_mode.help_text: The Nextflow `publishDir` option specifies which intermediate files should be saved to the output directory. This option tells the pipeline what method should be used to move these files. See [Nextflow docs](https://www.nextflow.io/docs/latest/process.html#publishdir) for details. Note that the SDBG directory is published with `--graph_publish_mode` instead, so that turning on `--save_mcaat_graph` does not copy tens of gigabytes per sample.

email_on_fail.description: Email address for completion summary, only when pipeline fails.
email_on_fail.help_text: An email address to send a summary email to when the pipeline is completed - ONLY sent if the pipeline does not exit successfully.

plaintext_email.description: Send plain-text email instead of HTML.

hook_url.description: Incoming hook URL for messaging service.
hook_url.help_text: Incoming hook URL for messaging service. Currently, MS Teams and Slack are supported.

trace_report_suffix.description: Suffix to add to the trace report filename. Default is the date and time in the format yyyy-MM-dd_HH-mm-ss.

pipelines_testdata_base_path.description: Base URL or local path to location of pipeline test dataset files
pipelines_testdata_base_path.help_text: The default points at RNABioInfo/mcaat-nf-testdata, which DOES NOT EXIST YET. It has to be created and populated before any `-profile test*` run can succeed. Until then, point this at a local checkout of the test data.

modules_testdata_base_path.description: Base URL or local path to location of modules test dataset files
